initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% cell position
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% exactly one colour per output cell (including 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy each nonzero input cell to the output (keep its colour)
output(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% propagate colours to neighbour rows (both directions) unless colour is 5
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R + 1, R1 < N.
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R - 1, R1 >= 0.

% row constraint: each row must have at most two nonzero (non5) cells
n_nonzero(R) :- N = #count{C : output(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color) != 0, Color != 5}.
violates(R) :- n_nonzero(R), N > 2.
:- not violates(R).   % rows with >2 such cells are forbidden

% show the result
#show output/3.

positive_N(N) :- true.

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy each nonzero input cell to the output (keep its colour)
output(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% propagate colours to neighbour rows (both directions) unless colour is 5
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R + 1, R1 < N.
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R - 1, R1 >= 0.

% row constraint: each row must have at most two nonzero (non5) cells
n_nonzero(R) :- N_R = #count{C : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5}.
violates(R) :- n_nonzero(R), N_R > 2.
:- violates(R).   % rows with >2 such cells are forbidden

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
positive_N(N) :- true.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% cell position
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% exactly one colour per output cell (including 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy each nonzero input cell to the output (keep its colour)
output(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% propagate colours to neighbour rows (both directions) unless colour is 5
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R + 1, R1 < N.
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R - 1, R1 >= 0.

% row constraint: each row must have at most two nonzero (non5) cells
n_nonzero(R) :- N_R = #count{C : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5}.
violates(R) :- n_nonzero(R), N_R > 2.
:- violates(R).   % rows with >2 such cells are forbidden

% show the result
#show output/3.

n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% cell position
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% exactly one colour per output cell (including 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% copy each nonzero input cell to the output (keep its colour)
output(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% propagate colours to neighbour rows (both directions) unless colour is 5
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R + 1, R1 < N.
output(R1,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5, R1 = R - 1, R1 >= 0.

% row constraint: each row must have at most two nonzero (non5) cells
n_nonzero(R) :- N_R = #count{C : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 5}.
violates(R) :- n_nonzero(R), N_R > 2.
